title: Merging Different Task Adapters
concept: Merging let you combine a coding adapter and a translation adapter into a single backbone, creating a multi-task generalist model.

exercises.prompt: What is an adapter in AI? What is a model adapter?

options.text: A small add-on that teaches a model a specific skill
A physical cable used to plug in a computer
A tool to delete the AI's entire memory

exercises.explain: An adapter is a small, lightweight set of weights added to a large model to teach it a specific new skill.

cards.title: Double the Superpowers!
cards.body: What if your AI is great at writing code, but terrible at speaking French? Or great at Spanish, but clueless about Python? Instead of choosing one or running two heavy models, what if we could just blend their skills together? Let's explore how we merge adapters!

cards.title: What is Adapter Merging?
cards.body: Adapter merging is the process of combining two or more specialized adapters (like one trained for coding and one for translation) directly into a single AI model. This creates a multi-task generalist model that can handle both skills without needing to switch between different adapter files.

cards.title: The Swiss Army Knife
cards.body: Think of adapters like snap-on tools. If you have a Lego robot, you can snap on a flashlight for dark rooms, and a magnet for picking up keys. Merging is like building a custom tool that has both the flashlight and the magnet built right in. Now, your robot can light up the room and pick up keys at the same exact time!

cards.title: Coding + Translation
cards.body: Imagine you have a base AI model. You train a 'Python Helper' adapter and a 'Spanish Translator' adapter. By merging them together into the base model, you get a single AI that can write Python code and explain how it works in Spanish, without needing to swap out the brain files!

cards.title: Do Skills Collide?
cards.body: People think that if you merge two adapters, they will fight and ruin each other's skills. In reality, thanks to smart math, we can blend them together so the AI keeps both skills intact without getting confused!

cards.title: Separate vs. Merged
cards.body: Instead of keeping your specialized skills separated in different files, merging brings them together into one unified brain.

right.text: One model that instantly writes code in French or Spanish!

left.text: Must load Coding Adapter OR Translation Adapter one at a time.

exercises.prompt: What do you create when you merge a coding adapter and a translation adapter into one backbone?

options.text: An adapter that only works for image generation

exercises.explain: A generalist model is created by merging different adapters (like coding and translation) into a single base model, allowing it to do multiple tasks.

exercises.prompt: True or False: You can merge adapters that were trained on completely different tasks.
exercises.explain: You can absolutely merge adapters trained on different tasks, like coding and translation, to make a versatile hybrid model.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe combining multiple adapters into one model:
exercises.sentence: By using adapter [blank], we can combine a coding specialist and a language specialist into one model.
exercises.explain: Adapter merging combines the parameters of multiple specialized adapters so a single model can perform multiple tasks at the same time.

exercises.prompt: Which AI helper correctly explains how merging two adapters works?

options.text: AI 1: "It blends the weights of both adapters so the model can code and translate in a single run."
AI 2: "It quickly deletes one adapter whenever you want to use the other one."
AI 3: "It breaks both adapters so the model forgets how to do both tasks."

exercises.explain: AI 1 is correct because merging combines the capabilities of both adapters into one, allowing it to translate and code simultaneously. AI 2 describes switching, not merging. AI 3 describes a negative interference that merging techniques aim to avoid.

exercises.prompt: Why would an engineer want to merge a coding adapter and a translation adapter instead of keeping them separate?

options.text: It saves storage space and computer power by running one unified model.
It makes the model run much slower but increases accuracy.
It requires you to delete the backbone model completely.
It only works if both adapters were trained on the exact same data.

exercises.explain: When you merge different adapters into one model, you save storage because you only load one model, and you save computer power because you don't have to run multiple separate models to get different tasks done!
